task_description: find all files accessed within the last day

trace.stderr:
find: paths must precede expression: `/path/to/search'
./tasks/task_194/step_3.sh: line 4: unexpected EOF while looking for matching ``'

messages.content:
```bash
find /path/to/search -type f -amin -1440
```

This command searches for files (`-type f`) in the specified directory and its subdirectories that were accessed within the last 24 hours (1440 minutes). Adjust `/path/to/search` to the directory you want to search.

execution trace:
stdout:

return code: 1

```bash
echo "/path/to/search" | xargs ls -l --time-style="+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"
```

This command lists the details of files in the specified directory, showing the last access time. Adjust `/path/to/search` to the directory you want to inspect.

execution trace:

stderr:

return code: 123

```bash
echo "/path/to/search" | xargs find . -type f -newermt $(date -d "1 day ago" +%Y-%m-%d)
```

This command finds files modified within the last day in the specified directory. Adjust `/path/to/search` to the directory you want to search.
